title: International Shared Support
subtitle: Institutional backing, international participation, and shared continuity for Sutton SignWriting
summary: A shared stewardship model for sustaining Sutton SignWriting as international language infrastructure rather than a single-maintainer project.
synopsis: Sutton SignWriting already supports international education, research, public language resources, and community use. Stewardship should combine an accountable host or fiscal-sponsor structure, international partner relationships, letters and statements of use, country-level coordination, and future grant, gift, donation, and collaboration pathways.

slides.title: Shared Language Infrastructure
slides.summary: Sutton SignWriting has international dependence, so its stewardship model should be international too.

image_prompts.concrete: International infrastructure slide showing SignWriting education, research, public language resources, and community use across connected countries
image_prompts.abstract: Shared language infrastructure spanning multiple local contexts

slides.speech: Sutton SignWriting is no longer only a personal project or a technical archive. It supports education, research, public language resources, and community use across countries. The stewardship model should match that reality.
slides.notes: Open with infrastructure reality, not outreach rhetoric.

slides.title: Participation Model
slides.summary: The model combines an accountable host or fiscal-sponsor structure, partner evidence, country coordination, and future funding pathways.

image_prompts.concrete: Participation model slide combining accountable host or fiscal sponsor, partner statements, country coordination, grants, gifts, donations, and collaboration pathways
image_prompts.abstract: Distributed participation coordinated through an accountable stewardship structure

slides.speech: The strongest model combines an accountable host or fiscal-sponsor structure with international partner statements, country-level coordination, and future grants, gifts, donations, or collaboration pathways. No single partner has to carry the whole structure.
slides.notes: Make this feel practical and distributed.

slides.title: How Partners Help
slides.summary: Partners can strengthen the case through evidence, introductions, proposals, and local context.

image_prompts.concrete: Partner participation slide showing statements of use, letters, institutional introductions, proposal collaboration, and local context
image_prompts.abstract: Local evidence and relationships strengthening a shared stewardship case

slides.speech: International partners can help by documenting local use, writing letters of support, introducing appropriate institutional contacts, collaborating on proposals, and explaining what support would make the work more useful in their own setting.

slides.title: Near-Term Actions
slides.summary: The practical next step is to collect bounded evidence, reusable statements, partner introductions, and candidate coordination paths.

image_prompts.concrete: Near-term action slide showing evidence collection, reusable partner statements, institutional introductions, candidate coordination paths, and proposal readiness
image_prompts.abstract: Concrete next steps moving from relationships to accountable stewardship

slides.speech: The near-term work is practical. Collect statements of use. Prepare reusable partner language. Identify appropriate institutional introductions. Map candidate coordination paths. These steps make future grants, gifts, donations, or fiscal-sponsor conversations more concrete.
slides.notes: Close with action without sounding like a campaign.
